findings.title: Passwords use a fast unsalted SHA-256 hash
Password verification does not use constant-time comparison
Reset tokens are checked for prior use but never consumed

reasoning: The repository fails because password storage uses unsalted SHA-256 rather than an approved password KDF, and password verification uses direct equality. Reset-token generation uses CSPRNG and reset-token comparison is constant time, but verification does not mark a valid token as used, so single use is not established. The reset route is rate-limited before registration and returns a generic response. No login, MFA verification, or development bypass route is present.
